title: Mega Garchomp Battle Guide
summary: 170 Attack nuke that pairs with [sand-force] — under sand, [earthquake] and [stone-edge] both gain a 30% boost on top of STAB.
strengths: 170 Attack is one of the highest Mega Attack stats

recommendedMoves.reason: Boosted [Ground] STAB — the main damage move

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Garchomp combines 170 base Attack with [sand-force], which boosts [Ground], [Rock], and [Steel] moves by 30% in sand. Under a sand team, [earthquake] becomes one of the strongest spread moves in the format, and [stone-edge] hits the [Flying] and [Fire] targets that [earthquake] misses.

## Strengths
170 Attack is among the highest on any Mega. [Dragon]/[Ground] dual STAB gives very wide coverage with few immune targets. 120 Special Attack also enables a mixed set with [draco-meteor] or [fire-blast] if needed. Under sand, Mega Garchomp KOs most threats through neutral hits alone.

## Weaknesses & Answers
4x [Ice] is the defining weakness — [ice-beam] users like [kyogre] or scarfed [mamoswine] end the set immediately. Speed 92 is slow for a glass-cannon attacker, so [tailwind] support matters. The pre-Mega turn loses [rough-skin] chip damage.

## Recommended Moveset
[earthquake] is locked in — with [sand-force] it becomes a spread nuke. [dragon-claw] is the reliable [Dragon] STAB. [stone-edge] is [sand-force]-boosted coverage. [protect] secures Mega evolution and sets up partner plays.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[garchompite] is mandatory. [sand-force] activates after Mega. Tera [Ground] boosts [earthquake] further, while Tera [Dragon] softens the [Ice] 4x weakness to 2x.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair with sand setters ([tyranitar], [hippowdon]) to activate [sand-force]. [Steel] or [Fire] partners cover the [Ice] weakness. [tailwind] support makes Mega Garchomp a reliable sweeper.

## Play Patterns
Enter after sand is up, Mega evolve behind [protect], then spam [earthquake] and [stone-edge] for spread damage. Use [dragon-claw] on [Dragon] targets and save Tera for an incoming [Ice] move.
